# Fix icon sequence and logic (2025-08-06 14:56Z)

_**User**_

the logic and sequence of the icons seems to be wrong, the icon sequence should follow what I provided in the design:
@https://www.figma.com/design/5GtceJXOtyBiwa7VG9i5q0/55380_PPL_October_Editoral?node-id=3-53&t=pOuAwE4dK1hg1zMY-1 

when user find a match, the card should stay open,with checkmark on top of it, there will be a tip related to the match display below (you can just use lorem ipsum for now), when user click on keep playing button, the checkmark disappears, but the card stays open: @https://www.figma.com/design/5GtceJXOtyBiwa7VG9i5q0/55380_PPL_October_Editoral?node-id=4-512&t=pOuAwE4dK1hg1zMY-1 

when it's not a match, the no-match cards stays open with cross on top of it, when user click on "keep playing", the unmatch cards flips back:
@https://www.figma.com/design/5GtceJXOtyBiwa7VG9i5q0/55380_PPL_October_Editoral?node-id=4-729&t=pOuAwE4dK1hg1zMY-1 

I already put the check and cross icons inside the images folder

+     
+     handleMatch(card1, card2) {
+         // Show checkmark overlays
+         card1.querySelector('.check-overlay').style.display = 'block';
+         card2.querySelector('.check-overlay').style.display = 'block';
+         
+         // Mark as matched
+         card1.classList.add('matched');
+         card2.classList.add('matched');
+         
+         // Get the tip for this match
+         const itemName = card1.dataset.item;
+         const item = this.energyItems.find(item => item.name === itemName);
+         this.currentTip = item ? item.tip : 'Great job finding a match!';
+         
+         // Show tip and keep playing button
+         this.showTip('It\'s a match!', this.currentTip);
+         
+         this.flippedCards = [];
+         this.matchedPairs++;
+         this.updateDisplay();
+         this.checkGameOver();
+     }
+     
+     handleNoMatch(card1, card2) {
+         // Show cross overlays
+         card1.querySelector('.cross-overlay').style.display = 'block';
+         card2.querySelector('.cross-overlay').style.display = 'block';
+         
+         // Mark as wrong
+         card1.classList.add('wrong');
+         card2.classList.add('wrong');
+         
+         // Show no match message and keep playing button
+         this.showTip('Oops, not a match. Try again.', 'Keep trying to find matching pairs!');
+     }
+     
+     showTip(title, tip) {
+         this.isShowingTip = true;
+         
+         // Update the text section
+         const textSection = document.querySelector('.text-section');
+         textSection.innerHTML = `
+             <h1 class="main-title">${title}</h1>
+             <p class="subtitle">${tip}</p>
+             <button class="keep-playing-btn" onclick="game.continueGame()">Keep Playing</button>
+         `;
+     }
+     
+     continueGame() {
+         this.isShowingTip = false;
+         
+         // Hide all overlays
+         document.querySelectorAll('.card-overlay').forEach(overlay => {
+             overlay.style.display = 'none';
+         });
+         
+         // Remove wrong class from cards and flip them back if not matched
+         this.cards.forEach(card => {
+             if (card.classList.contains('wrong')) {
+                 card.classList.remove('flipped', 'wrong');
+             }
+         });
+         
+         // Reset flipped cards
+         this.flippedCards = [];
+         
+         // Restore original text
+         const textSection = document.querySelector('.text-section');
+         textSection.innerHTML = `
+             <h1 class="main-title">Can you pick the places your home could save more energy?</h1>
+             <p class="subtitle">Match two and discover an easy tip to help you get started.</p>
+         `;
+     }

Now let me create a comprehensive summary of the changes I've made to fix the game logic and implement the correct sequence based on the Figma design:

## Summary of Changes Made

I've successfully implemented the correct game logic and sequence based on your Figma design requirements. Here are the key changes:

### 1. **Fixed Icon Sequence**
- Updated the `energyItems` array to follow the correct sequence from the Figma design:
  1. Curtain
  2. Fan  
  3. Window
  4. Fireplace
  5. Carpet
  6. Thermostat

### 2. **Implemented Match Behavior**
- When cards match:
  - Cards stay open with checkmark overlays on top
  - Tip related to the match is displayed below

- Created "Keep Playing" button styling to match Figma design
